markdown.markdown:
**Oregon State University** \| Corvallis, OR \| B.S. Marine Biology, Minor in Statistics \| CO 2026 ## Work experience **R-Ladies Corvallis** | Co-founder | January, 2026 - Present
  • Co-founded and helped organize a Corvallis chapter focused on building community among R users and hosting workshops and events for members.
  • Manage meeting coordination, planning, and outreach.
**Oregon State University** | Statistics Learning Assistant | September, 2024 - Present
  • Assisted students in grasping and applying statistical concepts for data-driven problem solving.
  • Demonstrated techniques and supported assignments using R.
  • Collaborated with instructors to improve course materials and enhance student learning experiences.
**Dutch Bros Coffee** | Barista/Shift Lead | May, 2024 - Present ## Relevant Coursework **Introduction to Statistical Methods I and II (ST 351, ST 352):** Study design, descriptive statistics, probability and sampling distributions, hypothesis testing, regression, ANOVA, and logistic regression. **Methods of Data Analysis I and II (ST 411, ST 412):** Graphical methods, parametric and nonparametric comparisons, ANOVA, simple and multiple linear regression, model diagnostics, and variable selection. **Data Visualization (ST 437):** Visual encoding, design principles, and ethical communication. Emphasis on critiquing and creating publication-ready visualizations. **Introduction to Mathematical Statistics I and II (ST 421, ST 422):** Probability theory, random variables, discrete and continuous distributions, sampling distributions, Central Limit Theorem, estimation, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing. **Use and Abuse of Data: Critical Thinking in Science (BDS 211):** Critical thinking in data analysis — evaluating logical arguments, identifying unstated assumptions, and recognizing deceptive visualizations, misleading comparisons, and spurious reasoning in the context of biological datasets. **Computer Programming (CS 201):** Fundamental programming concepts, computational thinking, and algorithmic problem-solving applied to real-world problems. **Foundations of Biological Data Sciences (BDS 310):** Scientific computing and data analysis in Python, including data visualization, reproducible research, and applied problem-solving with real-world life sciences datasets. **Applied Conservation Biology in Gorongosa National Park (Mozambique) (ENSC 399):** Hands-on fieldwork in ecological data collection, restoration monitoring, and conservation management. Used R to analyze and communicate field data and scientific findings. **Statistical Consulting Practicum (ST 407):** Observation of and participation in a statistical consulting practicum, gaining exposure to real-world data problems and client communication. ## Volunteer Experience **View Acres Elementary** | Classroom Aide | September, 2014 - May, 2020
  • Logged 500+ hours supporting 4th and 5th grade students in math, reading, and writing.
  • Worked directly with students during lessons to reinforce concepts and provide individualized support
  • Assisted teacher with classroom activities and helped maintain an engaging learning environment
